food_subgroup: Bread and buns — kopitiam-style soft buns

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (bread roll) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Singaporean kopitiam-style soft buns bundle.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E (Bread and rolls) is the closest defensible parent category. No specific code for kopitiam bun bundles.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific food item.

preparation_method: may be filled with kaya or butter

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a bundle of 5 soft kopitiam-style buns, each approximately 50 g, totalling approximately 250 g per bundle.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared (USDA SR Legacy 18069) and Bread roll, plain (HPB Singapore Foodo reference values for soft white rolls)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy); Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for white bread rolls (FDC ID 325871) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo values for plain soft white bread rolls. The buns bundle at Toast Box consists of soft white bread rolls typical of Singaporean kopitiam style. Values represent a plain soft white bun without filling; actual values may vary if kaya or butter filling is included. Per-100g values are used as the nutritional basis.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 71 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables for white bread (GI=71, glucose reference). Singaporean kopitiam soft buns are made from refined white flour with added sugar and fat, closely analogous to commercially prepared white bread rolls. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (71 × 132.5) / 100 = 94.1, rounded to 94.3 for 250 g serving (53 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.5 = 132.5 g carbohydrate per bundle).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~490 mg per 100 g; ~1225 mg per bundle of 5 buns)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a bundle of 5 buns contributes approximately 1225 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; portion control and avoidance of high-sodium fillings such as salted butter are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 71) and high carbohydrate load (~132.5 g carbohydrate per bundle)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of this high-GI refined white bread bundle, as the large carbohydrate load (approximately 132.5 g per bundle) will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; if consumed, portion should be restricted to one bun and paired with protein or fat to blunt glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate content and moderate sodium; low saturated fat in plain form but filling-dependent
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain soft white buns are low in saturated fat (~0.8 g per 100 g), but the high refined carbohydrate content and sodium level are relevant for cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose wholegrain alternatives where possible and avoid high-fat or high-sugar fillings such as kaya and butter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Buns Bundle at Toast Box is a popular Singaporean kopitiam (traditional coffee shop) offering consisting of a choice of 5 soft white bread buns, typically served as a snack or light meal accompaniment. Toast Box is a well-known Singaporean chain that recreates the traditional kopitiam experience. The buns are made from refined white flour enriched with sugar, butter or margarine, eggs, and milk, giving them a soft, slightly sweet, and pillowy texture characteristic of Asian-style milk buns. They may be served plain or with optional fillings such as kaya (a traditional Singaporean coconut and egg jam sweetened with sugar) or salted butter. Nutritionally, the buns are energy-dense, providing approximately 279 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (~53 g per 100 g). The glycemic index is high (GI ~71), making the bundle a significant glycemic load food when consumed as a full set of 5 buns (~250 g). Sodium content is notable at ~490 mg per 100 g. The buns are a staple of the Singaporean breakfast and tea-time culture and are widely recognised across all ethnic communities in Singapore. Clinically, they are of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to the high GI and large carbohydrate load, and for those with hypertension due to the sodium content. The bundle is vegetarian and halal-certified in most Toast Box outlets.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for Toast Box buns bundle as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy white bread roll data (FDC 325871) and cross-referenced with HPB Foodo plain white bread roll values. The adaptation is defensible as the product is a standard soft white bun, but actual values will vary depending on filling choice (plain, kaya, or butter). Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent a plain soft white bun without filling. If kaya or butter filling is included, energy, fat, sugar, and sodium values will be higher. 2. Serving size of 250 g (5 × 50 g buns) is an estimate; actual bun weight may vary by outlet. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this bread roll entry. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this bread roll entry. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this bread roll entry. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this bread roll entry. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for commercially prepared white bread rolls. 8. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain white bread rolls without egg-enriched filling. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as bread is not a source of vitamin C. 10. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate based on white bread from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement exists for Toast Box buns specifically. 11. The cuisine is classified as Singaporean Kopitiam reflecting the cultural context of Toast Box as a kopitiam-style chain.
